mapping.a_element: phases of the electromagnetic modes acting as Ising-like spins
mapping.b_element: heterogeneous economic agents as the interacting degrees of freedom

mapping.a_element: structural randomness / multiple-scattering disorder giving quenched random couplings
nonlinear mode competition in the gain medium
spin-glass phase: field phases frozen in an exponentially large number of configurations
mapping.b_element: long epochs of stasis arising from many metastable states

mapping.a_element: hyper-sensitivity / criticality near the spin-glass transition
mapping.b_element: hyper-sensitivity to small changes of the environment and crises

mapping.a_element: mean-field spin-glass Hamiltonian and its phase diagram
mapping.b_element: spin-glass models and agent-based simulations proposed for the economy

brief_justification: Both rest on the same spin-glass machinery: quenched disorder plus frustration/competition producing a rugged landscape with an exponentially large number of frozen/metastable states and hyper-sensitivity to perturbations. Paper A computes this explicitly (mean-field Hamiltonian, phase diagram, spin-glass phase of frozen field phases) for mode-locked lasers, while paper B advocates the identical spin-glass framework — competition + heterogeneity generating metastable stasis punctuated by crises and hyper-sensitivity — for economic systems, so a coherent role-for-role mapping holds.
